summary: Completed testing of Bookings Page Revamp feature. Backend APIs pass (10/10 tests). Frontend View Mode toggle working after expo restart. Admin Feedback tab bug (Platform is not defined) verified as FIXED.

ui_bugs.issue: data-testid attributes not being picked up by Playwright (view-mode-mentor, view-mode-client)
ui_bugs.note: Toggle visually renders correctly, only automated testing selector issue

critical_code_review_comments: View Mode toggle correctly renders for professional users
Booking card normalization handles both old (date/time_slot/amount) and new (scheduled_at/total_amount/service_title) formats
Tab filters (Upcoming/Pending/Completed/Cancelled) work correctly in both views

test_credentials.user_professional: jcuradeau.7@gmail.com / Crypto2026! (Note: This user is ALSO a professional, not a regular user)

features_verified.GET /api/bookings/my: PASS - Returns user's bookings as client
features_verified.PUT /api/pro/dashboard/bookings/{id}/sta: PASS - Allows mentor to update booking status
features_verified.Booking card with client_name: PASS - Shows 'from Test Client' in mentor view
features_verified.Confirm/Complete buttons: PASS - Visible in mentor view for pending/confirmed bookings
features_verified.Admin Feedback tab crash fix: PASS - No 'Platform is not defined' error

context_for_next_testing_agent: Both test users (admin@cryptonai.com and jcuradeau.7@gmail.com) are professionals with is_professional=true. The bookings page View Mode toggle only appears for professional users. The toggle shows Mentor View and Client View options. Mentor View calls /api/pro/dashboard/bookings, Client View calls /api/bookings/my. Old booking data has format {date, time_slot, amount} while new bookings have {scheduled_at, total_amount, service_title} - the normalize function handles both.
rca_of_issue: Initial test failure was due to expo not hot-reloading the bookings.tsx changes. After restarting expo, all features worked correctly.
